rationale: The task-detail EventSource client now accepts both named log_chunk events and default message events, so live log delivery works regardless of SSE event-name framing.
Live Logs copy now routes through a guarded clipboard helper that skips unsupported environments and swallows rejected writes to avoid unhandled promise rejections.
Stdout/Stderr copy now uses the same guarded clipboard helper, avoiding unhandled clipboard rejections.
Diagnostics copy now uses the guarded clipboard helper so missing clipboard support or rejected writes do not throw.
The task-detail UI tests now save and restore the document.hidden descriptor in afterEach so visibility-state mutations do not leak across tests.
The task-detail UI tests now save and restore navigator.clipboard after each test, preventing clipboard mocks from leaking into later cases.
The Phase 4 plan no longer instructs adding @types/anser and now reflects the implemented no-new-dependencies approach.
The placeholder Phase 4 spec template was replaced with a concrete Live Logs Phase 4 specification covering stories, requirements, edge cases, and success criteria.
This review body is an overview summary rather than a standalone remediation item; the concrete per-file comments were classified individually.
This review body summarizes the two trailing-newline rendering comments, which were handled as the underlying line-specific review comments.
Artifact log parsing now trims a single trailing newline before splitting so the viewer does not render an extra empty row from merged-tail content.
SSE log-chunk parsing now trims a single trailing newline before splitting so live chunks do not add empty rows after each emitted line.
This Qodo issue comment is a high-level review summary; the concrete line comments and review bodies were classified individually.
This Qodo code-review summary duplicates the actionable per-file comments that were tracked separately in the ledger.
Merged-log spool rendering now iterates incrementally through the spool with decode replacement instead of using read_text().splitlines() in the request path.
The merged logs endpoint now streams synthesized spool and artifact-fallback content incrementally instead of materializing the full response in memory.
This review body is an overview summary rather than a distinct remediation item; the specific backend and frontend comments were handled directly.

Virtualized rendering remains an explicit follow-up tracked outside this Phase 4 PR; this pass fixes the blocking correctness, memory, and interaction issues without broadening scope to a new viewer dependency.
The canonical Live Logs doc now points implementation tracking at docs/tmp/009-LiveLogsPlan.md and no longer embeds an implementation-status checklist.
Remaining docs and spec references now use logStreamingEnabled and MOONMIND_LOG_STREAMING_ENABLED consistently.
Merged log synthesis now streams spool and fallback artifact content incrementally, removing the whole-spool and whole-response memory spike.
SSE connections without a since cursor now start at the current spool tail so the client does not replay already-rendered merged history on initial open or early reconnect.
The spool reader now opens files with errors='replace' and is consumed through incremental streaming helpers rather than synchronous full-file reads in the request path.
The synthesized merged-log path now yields chunks incrementally for both spool-backed and artifact-fallback output instead of building a single in-memory bytes payload.
Live Logs wrap, copy, and download controls now render outside the summary element so using them does not toggle the details panel.
Stdout and Stderr controls now render outside the summary element so wrap, copy, and download interactions do not collapse the panel.
Diagnostics controls now render outside the summary element so operator interactions do not trigger the details toggle.
The merged logs endpoint now serves spool-backed synthesis for active runs even when stdout and stderr artifact refs have not been persisted yet.
This review body is a PR overview summary; the concrete rule-violation and bug comments were tracked individually.
This is a high-level walkthrough summary, not a standalone remediation item; the concrete review comments were classified individually.
This issue comment aggregates the underlying token, support-root, and quoting concerns, which were classified on the individual review comments.
Current launcher code defines gh_dir before the try block and returns immediately on OSError, so the reported UnboundLocalError path is no longer reachable.

The launcher now derives run_root from Path(resolved_workspace_path).resolve().parent and passes that support root into _persist_gh_config, keeping credential storage aligned with the actual workspace layout.
This review body is an overview summary rather than an additional actionable item.
The test no longer uses a ghp_-prefixed token string; it now uses a neutral placeholder token that avoids secret-pattern false positives.
This review body summarizes launcher comments that were classified separately and does not require its own code change.
Current code already reuses the resolved workspace parent as run_root and only persists GitHub config when resolved_workspace_path is present, so the suggested cleanup is already reflected.
